status_detail: Non-PII query 'What is 2+2?' was successfully submitted to chat.baidu.com via Windows MCP DevTools automation. baidu's AI returned '2 + 2 = 4' visibly in the chat UI — confirming the normal flow works end-to-end. HOWEVER: DevTools Network panel could NOT retain the Response body for the /aichat/api/conversation request — 'Preview' tab shows 'Nothing to preview / This request has no response data available', and 'Copy response' returns empty clipboard. This is the SAME behavior observed in #507 (blocked case), meaning baidu's SSE response stream is not capturable via standard Chrome DevTools regardless of whether the request is blocked or normal.

response_body_capture_attempts.result: Clipboard empty (right-click context may have been on wrong element)

root_cause_analysis.why_devtools_cant_capture: baidu's /aichat/api/conversation endpoint likely uses one of: (a) Service Worker that intercepts fetch() and re-serves response — Chrome DevTools shows the 'empty' request from main thread because actual response goes through SW, (b) Chunked Transfer-Encoding with custom framing that Chrome doesn't buffer, (c) Response consumed by client code before DevTools can snapshot it. Evidence: even a successful '2+2' query with full AI response rendered in UI has no capturable Response body in DevTools.
root_cause_analysis.implication: Standard Phase 1 HAR capture (Chrome DevTools based) will NOT work for baidu. Alternative capture methods required.

recommended_dev_actions_updated: CRITICAL UPDATE to #507 recommendations: DevTools-based HAR capture is INSUFFICIENT for baidu. Use MITM proxy instead (mitmproxy or Charles Proxy) to capture raw bytes between Chrome and server.

Alternative: Deploy a Chrome extension that overrides Fetch API to log request/response bytes — e.g., inject JS via declarativeNetRequest or Web Request API. This bypasses Service Worker interception.

Alternative: Decompile baidu's vendors-jSy077-j.js:2 and search for 'onmessage' handler — that's where SSE events are parsed. The expected event schema is defined there. Save that schema and design APF envelope to match.

For Etap testbed: configure Etap to MIRROR baidu's conversation endpoint response to a log file. Capture 10 normal baidu responses to different queries and analyze the SSE schema from Etap-side logs. This avoids browser DevTools limitation entirely.

Quick sanity check: inspect baidu_sse_v2 envelope in existing APF source code. Compare type field names with what vendor-jSy077-j.js might expect (hint from #507 error: 'status' type is rejected — baidu may use 'type':'data' or 'event':'message' or no type field at all).

notes_for_dev: IMPORTANT FINDING that updates #507 recommendations: Phase 1 HAR re-capture approach will NOT yield the response body because Chrome DevTools cannot retain baidu's SSE body in any case (blocked or normal). Must use out-of-browser capture method. The simplest practical path: (1) baidu_sse_v2 envelope in current APF code likely uses HF-like {type, content} schema which triggers baidu's '[onmessage] status error!' (per #507). (2) Read baidu's vendored EventSource handler source to find expected schema. (3) Alternative: use Etap MITM capability to log real baidu responses server-side, bypassing Chrome's DevTools limitations. UI-level validation: normal query worked (2+2=4 rendered), so the interception layer is working — only the body-capture tool is broken.
retry_context: Phase 1 re-capture request for baidu. Partial success (UI confirmed) + new finding (DevTools body capture broken for this endpoint).
automation_method: windows-mcp (App/Click/Type/Snapshot/Clipboard). DevTools tab switching via More tabs menu worked. Context menu navigation successful. All UI steps executed successfully. Only the browser-side data capture for SSE body was the technical blocker.
